title: coprosma spathulata subsp. hikuruana
intro: Coprosma spathulata subsp. hikuruana, also known as the Hikuruana coprosma, is a rare and endemic plant species native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The Hikuruana coprosma has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. In traditional Maori medicine, the Hikuruana coprosma is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
